title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal comes from new work on model failure modes, internal safety monitoring, fairness operations, privacy accountability, and defenses against deepfake misuse. The mix leans practical: several papers propose concrete mechanisms for detecting harmful behavior, auditing real-world disclosures, or hardening systems against known abuse pathways rather than restating high-level principles.

items.title: Different Paths to Harmful Compliance: Behavioral Side Effects and Mechanistic Divergence Across LLM Jailbreaks
items.why_it_matters: Shows unsafe fine-tuning routes can produce distinct internal failure modes, complicating evaluation and mitigation.

items.title: LLM Safety From Within: Detecting Harmful Content with Internal Representations
items.why_it_matters: Suggests guard models can improve harmful-content detection by using safety signals from internal layers.

items.title: Moving beyond Principles: Identifying Actionable AI Fairness Practices
items.why_it_matters: Offers lifecycle-level fairness practices that could make governance requirements more operational and auditable.

items.title: Do Privacy Policies Match with the Logs? An Empirical Study of Privacy Disclosure in Android Application Logs
items.why_it_matters: Tests whether disclosed data practices align with observed logging behavior, a core privacy accountability issue.

items.title: MetaCloak-JPEG: JPEG-Robust Adversarial Perturbation for Preventing Unauthorized DreamBooth-Based Deepfake Generation
items.why_it_matters: Targets a concrete image-based abuse case by making anti-deepfake protections survive common compression.

items.title: Bounded Ratio Reinforcement Learning
items.why_it_matters: Improves stability in policy optimization, relevant where RL methods shape model behavior and safety tuning.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
